danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites with distinctive designs; minibosses that kneel and fire energy blasts

danger_signals.meaning: Stationary damage sources requiring navigation or destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Flying drones/enemies across level space, aircraft silhouettes (Sea Ray, Mamba canopy graphics)
danger_signals.meaning: Vertical threat requiring evasion or counter-fire

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual charging animation before three-blast burst from kneeling enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Attack telegraph allowing player reaction window

safety_signals.visual_form: Unlocked character roster progression (General Hawk, Duke, Spirit, etc.)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition through stage completion enables advancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden environmental objects marked by rapid chime sound effect
safety_signals.meaning: Safe collectibles for power-up or lore discovery

safety_signals.visual_form: Exit doors (some stages feature multiple exits indicating branching routes)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: G.I. Joe military insignia implied through character designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Character costume design reflects 1992 action figure lineup (Hawk with headset/jetpack reference, Wet Suit aquatic gear, Road Block distinctive silhouette)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cobra sigil placeholder graphics (cobra design graphics found in ROM as development markers)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy archetypes: bouncing minibosses, kneeling gunners, flying drones, aircraft (Sea Ray, Mamba), Cesspool boss with sword-swipe attacks
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: COBRA military installations implied through turrets, missile systems, aircraft hangars (canopy graphics suggest indoor/base environments), submarine/sea-based assets (Sea Ray aquatic vehicle)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opening/ending cinematics depicting Atlantis rising and sinking (glitched sound effect in unused audio suggests technical iteration on this sequence)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stages represent different geographical/infiltration routes to Atlantis; branching stage routes indicate player choice in approach method

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dialogue tree indicates Duke is wounded and requires Spirit character rescue (unused dialogue: 'I'VE BEEN WOUNDED AND I'M TOO WEAK TO MOVE. FIND SPIRIT AND BRING HIM HERE')
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage accessibility gated by character roster; rescuing Duke on snowy mountain unlocks his participation; Spirit rescue prerequisite blocks other missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character select screen shows available operatives; starting as General Hawk signals campaign opening; subsequent character availability reflects story advancement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each character retains upgrades across stage selections (except health), showing persistent loadout state; multi-exit stages represent strategic route choices

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Snowy mountain terrain (Duke's location), water environments (Wet Suit unused swimming sprites suggest aquatic stage), indoor COBRA bases (upward turrets, canopy graphics suggest hangar spaces)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character tools match environment (Wet Suit for water, aerial units for sky combat, standard infantry for base infiltration)

environmental_storytelling.example: COBRA bases feature turrets, missile systems, aircraft storage (Sea Ray/Mamba hangars with canopy graphics) — visual language of hostile military installation
General Hawk's jetpack and headset (referenced in manual despite 1992 figure not including them) signal command/reconnaissance role; Wet Suit's aquatic gear implies submarine/coastal missions

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage geography as mission type indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Snowy mountain = rescue extraction; water areas = infiltration; base interiors with turrets = stronghold assault

environmental_storytelling.technique: Unused assets as design iteration traces
environmental_storytelling.example: Early Road Block sprites, alternate Wet Suit swimming poses, canopied aircraft variants visible in ROM suggest developmental changes to mission structure and environmental interaction
Opening/ending cutscenes depicting Atlantis rising/sinking establish apocalyptic stakes; password system complexity implies high-stakes campaign structure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Kneels in place, fires three-blast energy burst; unused sprite shows windup animation intended to telegraph attack
Bounces back from hand-to-hand attacks; two variants designed but neither used in final game, suggesting combat system iteration
Flies across level space; fires missiles; multiple canopy variants suggest different aircraft types with distinct behaviors
Leaps at player and swipes with sword; unused sound effect suggests audio design polish for this specific attack pattern
Stationary, dialogue-based rescue trigger; dialogue indicates weakness state and dependency on ally Spirit for revival
Begins all playthroughs at base stats with no upgrades; retains progression across stages in debug mode, showing persistent character state

notes: Source data heavily weighted toward development/unused assets rather than in-game visual systems. Limited documentation on active manifestation mechanics. Inferred faction communication primarily through character/enemy design and environmental context. Password system complexity suggests world-state persistence across extended play sessions, though visual state-to-appearance mapping not explicitly documented. Multi-route stage design implies strategic player choice reflected in mission approach but lacking specific visual branching documentation.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - G.I. Joe: The Atlantis Factor)
